history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible up and to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. The cell to my right is open floor, so the safest next step is to turn right toward that path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D. That cell is floor and safe to enter. The goal is visible at row 05 column B, but moving forward first is the safest immediate step because it advances me through open space while avoiding the lava at row 07 column C and row 05 column C.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my left and slightly ahead. I am facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor tile. Turning toward the goal immediately would face lava on the left path, so the safest next step is to move forward onto the open floor and approach from above.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly to my left, but there is a lava cell between my current position and the goal, so moving toward it is unsafe. The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. My only safe immediate option is to turn and look for another path around the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible two rows ahead and aligned with one column to my left: from my position at row 07 column D, the goal is at row 05 column D. The cell directly in front of me is lava at row 06 column D, so moving forward would be deadly. The cell to my left is safe floor at row 07 column C, and turning left is the best first step to go around the lava toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is free floor, so moving forward is safe. The goal is visible to the right at row 07 column F, but the adjacent right cell at row 07 column E is lava, so I should not turn toward it yet. Advancing forward keeps me safe and may allow a path around the lava to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is empty floor, so moving forward is safe. The goal is not yet visible, and moving forward will explore the open corridor while avoiding the lava at row 06 column E.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is a wall at row 06 column D, so moving forward is blocked. To my right there is lava at row 07 column E, while to my left at row 07 column C there is a wall. Since both side cells are currently not enterable and forward is blocked, I should turn to search for another direction. I will turn right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible up ahead to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The cell to my right is an open floor tile, which can help me route around the lava toward the goal. I should turn right first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D facing upward, so the cell directly in front of me is row 06 column D, which is a safe floor tile. The goal is visible at row 05 column B, but moving straight first is safe and keeps options open. Turning is unnecessary because forward is not blocked by a wall or lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible one row ahead and two columns to the left relative to my current position. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, while the goal is not straight ahead yet. Moving forward into the safe cell preserves options and avoids the lava to the left-front area.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is immediately to my left, but there is lava between us at the cell directly left of my position. The cell in front of me is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. Turning left is the best next step to begin reorienting and look for a safe path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 07 column D facing upward. The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The open safe cell is to the left at row 07 column C, while the goal is up-left but blocked by lava straight ahead. I should turn left to face the safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my right on row 07 column F, while the cell directly in front of me is safe floor at row 06 column D. The cell to my immediate right is lava, so turning right would point toward danger. Moving forward is the safest progress, and from there I can approach the goal without stepping onto lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, with a wall boundary around the visible room. The cell directly in front of me is clear floor, so moving forward is safe. The goal is not yet visible, and moving forward explores the open area without stepping onto lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
